categories.name: Technology
categories.description: Engineering and technical delivery roles that build, operate, and improve digital systems.

categories.name: Product
categories.description: Roles that translate customer, market, and business signals into product decisions.

categories.name: Data
categories.description: Analytics and intelligence roles that convert raw information into decisions and operating insight.

categories.name: Business
categories.description: Strategy, operations, growth, and customer-facing roles that turn execution into business outcomes.

capabilities.name: API and system design
capabilities.description: Designs reliable services, data contracts, integrations, and operational boundaries.

capabilities.name: Software delivery
capabilities.description: Ships maintainable code through testing, review, iteration, and production feedback.

capabilities.name: Product requirement reasoning
capabilities.description: Turns ambiguous needs into scoped requirements, tradeoffs, and measurable outcomes.

capabilities.name: User and market discovery
capabilities.description: Uses qualitative and quantitative signals to identify customer problems and adoption risks.

capabilities.name: SQL and analytical modeling
capabilities.description: Builds datasets, metrics, and analysis flows that answer business questions accurately.

capabilities.name: Dashboard and metric communication
capabilities.description: Presents metrics with clear definitions, caveats, and action implications.

capabilities.name: Stakeholder alignment
capabilities.description: Aligns priorities, decisions, and tradeoffs across teams with different incentives.

capabilities.name: Business operations analysis
capabilities.description: Finds process bottlenecks, defines operating metrics, and improves execution cadence.

capabilities.name: Go-to-market analysis
capabilities.description: Connects customer segments, channels, positioning, and revenue signals to execution plans.

capabilities.name: AI workflow fluency
capabilities.description: Applies AI tools and model outputs with structured evaluation, risk awareness, and human review.

templates.title: Backend Engineer
templates.summary: Builds APIs, services, data flows, and production systems that support product and business workflows.
templates.business_context: Technology role in a product-led environment with reliability, delivery quality, and cross-functional execution expectations.
responsibilities: Design and implement backend APIs, service logic, and data access patterns.
Improve reliability, observability, and performance of production services.
Collaborate with product and frontend partners to clarify requirements and integration contracts.
Write tests, review code, and document technical decisions for maintainability.

evidence_examples: Designed REST or GraphQL APIs with versioning, validation, and error handling.
Owned a service integration with clear data contracts and monitoring.
common_gaps: Only lists frameworks without system design tradeoffs.
No evidence of production debugging or reliability ownership.
development_actions: Build a small service with documented API contracts, tests, logging, and failure cases.
Write a design note explaining data model, scaling assumptions, and tradeoffs.

evidence_examples: Shipped features through tests, review, deployment, and post-release validation.
Reduced defects or cycle time through refactoring or tooling improvements.
common_gaps: Project descriptions stop at implementation and omit validation.
No mention of tests, code review, or operational feedback.
development_actions: Add unit and integration tests to a representative project.
Document one production-like issue and how it was diagnosed.

evidence_examples: Negotiated API scope or delivery sequence with product and frontend teams.
Clarified ambiguous requirements before implementation.
common_gaps: Evidence is purely solo technical work.
Tradeoffs with non-engineering stakeholders are not visible.
development_actions: Convert a project into a requirement-to-implementation case study.
Practice explaining technical decisions in business impact terms.

common_gaps: Thin production evidence beyond course or demo projects.
Insufficient explanation of tradeoffs, reliability, and ownership.
development_actions: Create one deployable backend project with tests, observability notes, and API documentation.
Prepare two stories covering debugging, tradeoff decisions, and measurable impact.

templates.title: AI Product Analyst
templates.summary: Connects user problems, product requirements, AI capabilities, and business metrics into evidence-based recommendations.
templates.business_context: AI-enabled product team where explainability, experimentation, and responsible use of model outputs matter.
responsibilities: Translate product questions into structured analysis plans and AI workflow requirements.
Evaluate user behavior, funnel metrics, and qualitative feedback to shape roadmap decisions.
Partner with engineering, design, and business teams to define success metrics and launch criteria.
Communicate recommendations with assumptions, evidence quality, risks, and confidence.

evidence_examples: Defined product requirements from ambiguous user or business problems.
Prioritized roadmap options using impact, effort, risk, and evidence quality.
common_gaps: Recommendations are generic and not tied to user behavior or business goals.
No clear success metric or prioritization logic.
development_actions: Write a PRD-style brief for an AI feature with metrics, constraints, and launch risks.
Build a prioritization table comparing three product bets.

evidence_examples: Designed an AI-assisted workflow with human review and evaluation criteria.
Compared model outputs using structured rubrics and failure analysis.
common_gaps: Uses AI terminology without evaluation or risk framing.
No evidence of prompt, output, or quality control design.
development_actions: Create an evaluation rubric for an AI feature and test sample outputs.
Document model failure modes and product mitigations.

evidence_examples: Analyzed activation, retention, conversion, or quality metrics.
Built a metrics view that changed a product decision.
common_gaps: Analysis lacks metric definitions or decision impact.
No connection between data and product recommendation.
development_actions: Create a sample funnel analysis with metric definitions and caveats.
Turn one analysis into a recommendation memo with confidence scoring.

common_gaps: AI claims are not supported by evaluation evidence.
Product recommendations lack prioritization and measurable outcomes.
development_actions: Build an AI feature case study with requirements, evaluation, and risk controls.
Prepare one analytics story that directly changed a roadmap or workflow decision.

templates.title: Data Analyst
templates.summary: Builds trusted analysis, metrics, and reporting that help teams make better product and business decisions.
templates.business_context: Data role embedded with product or business teams where insight quality and stakeholder adoption are both important.
responsibilities: Write SQL and transform data to answer product, customer, and operating questions.
Build dashboards and recurring reporting with clear metric definitions.
Investigate trends, anomalies, and experiment results to guide decisions.
Communicate findings with assumptions, limitations, and recommended next actions.

evidence_examples: Built SQL analysis across multiple tables with joins, windows, and clean metric definitions.
Diagnosed a metric movement and recommended a concrete business action.
common_gaps: Only mentions tools without analysis depth.
No caveats, assumptions, or metric definitions.
development_actions: Create a portfolio analysis with SQL, charts, assumptions, and recommendation.
Practice explaining one metric from source table to decision.

evidence_examples: Created a dashboard that reduced manual reporting or improved decision speed.
Defined metric ownership, refresh cadence, and interpretation guidance.
common_gaps: Dashboard screenshots lack business interpretation.
No evidence that stakeholders used the output.
development_actions: Add a short decision guide to an existing dashboard.
Document stakeholder questions each metric answers.

evidence_examples: Scoped analysis with stakeholders before querying data.
Explained a data limitation and aligned on a practical decision.
common_gaps: Analysis appears disconnected from stakeholder action.
No evidence of translating findings for non-technical audiences.
development_actions: Write an executive summary for a technical analysis.
Practice turning ambiguous questions into analysis plans.

common_gaps: Weak link between analysis output and business decision.
Insufficient treatment of data quality and assumptions.
development_actions: Build one end-to-end analytics case with SQL, visualization, interpretation, and action.
Prepare a story about handling messy or incomplete data.

templates.title: Business Operations Analyst
templates.summary: Improves operating rhythm, process quality, and business performance through analysis, coordination, and execution systems.
templates.business_context: Business operations role working across teams to make execution measurable, repeatable, and easier to manage.
responsibilities: Analyze operational processes, business metrics, and execution bottlenecks.
Build reports and operating cadences that clarify priorities and accountability.
Partner with sales, product, support, finance, or leadership teams on improvement initiatives.
Track outcomes and recommend changes based on evidence and business impact.

evidence_examples: Mapped a process, found a bottleneck, and improved cycle time or quality.
Created an operating metric that helped leaders manage execution.
common_gaps: Work is described as coordination without measurable operational improvement.
No baseline, intervention, or result.
development_actions: Document a process improvement with before and after metrics.
Create a simple operating dashboard for a recurring workflow.

evidence_examples: Used spreadsheet, BI, or SQL analysis to prioritize operational fixes.
Compared segments, trends, or funnel steps to identify leverage points.
common_gaps: Analysis is not tied to an operating decision.
No visibility into data handling or metric logic.
development_actions: Create an operational analysis with metric definitions and recommendation.
Practice explaining how a data cut changes the action plan.

evidence_examples: Aligned multiple teams around ownership, milestones, and tradeoffs.
Managed a recurring business review or execution cadence.
common_gaps: Cross-functional work lacks decision ownership.
No evidence of conflict resolution or prioritization.
development_actions: Prepare a case story about aligning stakeholders with different incentives.
Build a one-page operating review with owners, metrics, and next actions.

common_gaps: Business impact is too vague or activity-oriented.
Stakeholder coordination lacks evidence of decisions and accountability.
development_actions: Quantify one operations project with baseline, action, result, and tradeoff.
Practice communicating operating metrics to both executives and execution teams.
